data.body: FORCE_CLOSE: close gate(s) bypassed by user approval — no completion/signature attestation was signed. Reason: "All 10 children closed (S1,S2,S3,S4a,S5,S4b,S6-pre,S6,S7,S8). The ENTIRE topology was built + proven LIVE end-to-end on the running AWS box (i-00880b2c7f13527c5, rebar.solutions.navateam.com): provision (S1) -> Gerrit+review-bot behind nginx/TLS (S2) -> LLM-Review label+submit-req (S3) -> bot identity+webhooks+events-log (S4a) -> review_code->LLM-Review vote, the proven pipe + /rerun (S4b, change #101 PASS: vote==MAX->submit->MERGED->replicated to GitHub) -> Gerrit->GitHub non-force replication via deploy key (S5) -> the deploy-key-only GitHub ruleset (S6, APPLIED + EMPIRICALLY PROVEN to reject human push/force-push/PR-merge(--admin)/tag/deletion) -> e2e + monitoring(SNS delivery proven) + quiesced restore drill (RTO ~126s) + runbooks (S7) -> client walkthrough (S8). The 2 unmet epic ACs (repo PERMANENTLY locked; e2e on  with permanent direct-push rejection) are the DELIBERATE consequence of the operator's explicit apply-prove-rollback choice (confirmed via question): the lock + main-advance were proven then rolled back so the team's live repo/workflow is unchanged; the e2e ran on a deletable test branch + the lock-rejection was proven transiently in S6. The full permanent-cutover playbook (github_repository_ruleset IaC + apply/rollback scripts, feature toggles, mirror banner, Gerrit-only CONTRIBUTING workflow) is committed in infra/ + the runbooks for the real cutover. Closed-without-signature reflects the intentionally-deferred permanent cutover, not a capability gap.". Session: 19ba620e.
